mcq.question: What is an automated system?
wrong: A system that requires constant human input
A system that only stores and retrieves data
A system that connects devices together

mcq.question: What are the three main parts of an automated system?
What does processing usually involve in an automated system?
mcq.correct: Comparing sensor data to stored values
wrong: Sending data to an output device

mcq.question: What provides the input in an automated system?
What carries out the output in an automated system?
What does the processor do in an automated system?
How do sensors, processors and actuators work together?
mcq.correct: Sensors collect data, processor decides, actuator acts
wrong: Processor collects data, sensors act, actuator stores data
Actuator collects data, processor displays output, sensor stores it
Sensors store data, actuator processes it, processor outputs it

mcq.question: Which is an example of an automated system?
wrong: A computer used for typing documents
A phone being used to send messages

mcq.question: Which scenario best describes an automated system?
mcq.correct: A thermostat turning heating on when temperature drops
wrong: A user typing on a keyboard
A person manually switching on a heater

mcq.question: Which is an example of automation in transport?
wrong: A driver manually steering a car

mcq.question: What is one advantage of automated systems?
What is one disadvantage of automated systems?
mcq.correct: They may fail if sensor data is wrong
wrong: They can be expensive to set up
They may not handle unexpected situations well
